title: Binary Search
statement: Given a sorted array of integers nums and an integer target, return the index of target in nums, or -1 if it is not present. You must run in O(log n) time.

steps.description: Recognize the sorted input enables halving the search space at each step.
Set the loop invariant: if target is anywhere in the array, it is within [lo, hi].
Compute mid carefully (lo + (hi - lo) // 2 to avoid overflow in languages where it matters).
Decide loop termination: continue while lo <= hi; on each iteration shrink to [lo, mid-1] or [mid+1, hi].
Handle the target-not-present case: when the loop exits without a match, return -1.
State complexity: O(log n) time, O(1) space.
